title: Clermontia micrantha
taxonomy: Clermontia micrantha is classified within the family Asparagaceae. It belongs to the genus Clermontia, which is native to Hawaii. The species Clermontia micrantha is also known as the small-flowered Clermontia. This classification places it within the monocot group of flowering plants. Clermontia micrantha is a type of flowering plant that is native to Hawaii and is often found in high-altitude mountainous regions.

intro: Clermontia micrantha, also known as Hawaiian Hedyotis or Clermontia, is a flowering plant species native to Hawaii, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of fever, cough, and sore throats, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Clermontia micrantha include clerosterol, clerosterone, and other flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical applications and salves for wound healing and skin care.
